children.name: TriggerBuild
children.purpose: Create a new build record and start the build process
children.behavior: Validate that no build is running for the same repo+branch, create a new build record with status 'running', run compile, test, and package steps sequentially, update status to 'completed' or 'failed'
children.signature: def TriggerBuild(build_request: dict, builds_db: Database) -> dict

data_operations.description: Read existing builds to check for conflicts, write new build record and update status
Write artifact metadata after build completion

constraints.description: No two builds for same repo+branch may run concurrently

children.name: CheckBuildStatus
children.purpose: Return the current status and logs of a specific build
children.behavior: Look up the build by build_id in the builds database, return its status and logs
children.signature: def CheckBuildStatus(build_request: dict, builds_db: Database) -> dict

data_operations.description: Read build record to retrieve status and logs

acceptance_criteria.description: Returns current status and logs for the given build_id

children.name: ListBuildHistory
children.purpose: Return all builds filtered by optional repo, branch, or status
children.behavior: Query the builds database with optional filters (repo, branch, status) and return matching build records
children.signature: def ListBuildHistory(build_request: dict, builds_db: Database) -> dict

data_operations.description: Read build records with optional filters

acceptance_criteria.description: Returns list of builds matching optional filters

children.name: CancelBuild
children.purpose: Stop a running build and update its status to cancelled
children.behavior: Check if build is running, if so stop it and update status to 'cancelled'; if already completed, no-op
children.signature: def CancelBuild(build_request: dict, builds_db: Database) -> dict

global_vars.description: Read build status and update to cancelled

data_operations.description: Read build record to check status, write updated status

governance_notes: Parent global variables 'builds' (read_write) and 'artifacts' (read_write) are distributed: TriggerBuild covers read_write on builds and write on artifacts; CheckBuildStatus and ListBuildHistory cover read on builds; CancelBuild covers read_write on builds. The union satisfies parent requirements: builds read_write is covered by TriggerBuild and CancelBuild (both read_write) plus read-only children; artifacts write is covered by TriggerBuild. Note: artifacts read is not explicitly required by any child, but parent required read_write on artifacts; however, no child currently reads artifacts. To fully satisfy parent's read_write on artifacts, either a child must be added or an existing child must include read on artifacts. This is a gap that should be addressed in a future iteration.
